title: Coniogramme pilosa
taxonomy: Coniogramme pilosa is classified as a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It is a member of the genus Coniogramme, which contains a small number of species. Within the family Pteridaceae, Coniogramme pilosa is part of the order Polypodiales. This classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other ferns. Coniogramme pilosa is a vascular plant, characterized by its fern-like leaves and reproductive structures.
uses: Coniogramme pilosa is known for its potential medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used in various cultures for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It is also believed to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help prevent infections and promote wound healing. Furthermore, Coniogramme pilosa has been studied for its potential to treat various health conditions, including fever and digestive issues. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential benefits.

intro: Coniogramme pilosa, also known as the hairy coniogramme, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, native to East Asia and the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Coniogramme pilosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. Coniogramme pilosa is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and capsules, which are commonly used in traditional Asian medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including arthritis, fever, and respiratory infections.
